conditions.text: could we make this an IPC stream and not an IPC file? Then we could stream results to client, which might be nice if it's long-running. Should be done as part of this PR since it would be hard to change later.
this doesn't seem to be right. We should support u8 (binary) vectors with hamming distance. Can be done as a follow up though.
it would be cool to stream out results as they come in. Can be done in the future though.

files.patch:
@@ -39,6 +39,7 @@ arrow-ord = "57.2"
 arrow-schema = "57.2"
 arrow-select = "57.2"
 arrow-cast = "57.2"
+arrow-row = "57.2"
 async-trait = "0"
 datafusion = { version = "52.1", default-features = false }
 datafusion-catalog = "52.1"

@@ -119,7 +119,8 @@ abstract analyzeIndex(name, options?): Promise<RecordBatch<any>>
 Analyze a vector index by sweeping ANN parameters against exhaustive
 ground truth on a random sample of queries drawn from the table.
 
-Supports all IVF index variants on local tables.
+Supports all IVF index variants. Works against local and remote tables;
+on remote tables the computation runs server-side.
 
 The returned record batch has one row per `(nprobes, refine_factor, ef, k)`
 configuration with columns `num_partitions`, `index_type`, `k`, `nprobes`,
